name: Raymond Scott
description: American composer, bandleader, pianist, record producer and inventor (1908-1994)
abstract: Raymond Scott (born Harry Warnow; September 10, 1908 – February 8, 1994) was an American composer, band leader, pianist and record producer. Known best in his time as a composer of production music, Scott is today regarded as an early pioneer of electronica.

works_created.t: Suite No. 1 for Violin and Piano

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: I joined the Raymond Scott band. He was auditioning pianists and I took my shot. ... I joined his quintet and then it became a big band and we went on tour.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: See I was one of the first black drummers to be on staff at CBS... there with Raymond, because we were doing the Perry Como show at that time... Raymond, of course, as you know was a superb musician... we used to play the blues, Good Morning Blues program

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: I also played with Raymond Scott. ... I played with Raymond Scott in New York, Raymond Scott's band, the studio band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was on The Bell Telephone Hour, and The Hit Parade with Raymond Scott for a number of years I did. I freelanced.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: When I went in that band... Raymond Scott and Webster had black and white... he had Ben Webster on tenor

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: when I was with a band at one time by the name of Raymond Scott
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998
